review_text.en:
I visited the Swaminarayan Temple recently, and it was an amazing experience. The architecture is absolutely stunning – every pillar and sculpture is crafted with so much detail. It feels like a perfect blend of devotion and art.

The entire place is very clean and well-maintained. I really liked how calm and peaceful the environment was. The Abhishek ritual gave me a sense of deep spiritual connection. The staff was polite and helpful throughout.

There’s also a small food counter where you can get simple and tasty vegetarian meals. It’s a great place to visit with family or even alone if you want some peace of mind.

Highly recommended if you’re looking for a spiritual break from the busy city life!

The BAPS Swaminarayan Temple in Pune is a magnificent spiritual and cultural center that embodies the teachings and values of the Swaminarayan faith. The temple showcases stunning architectural design, intricate carvings, and exquisite craftsmanship.
There is large parking space for both 2 and 4 wheelers to accommodate vehicles on weekends. Few things for children to play. The food canteen was good with a number of snacks items. One can go in the evening  and see the lighting of the temple as well.Everyone must visit.

review_text.hi:
स्वामीनारायण मंदिर का स्थान सुंदर है और मंदिर की डिज़ाइन व वास्तुकला वास्तव में आकर्षक है। परिसर में पहुँचते ही आध्यात्मिक वातावरण महसूस होता है। हालाँकि, मंदिर तक जाने का रास्ता काफी संकरा और भीड़भाड़ वाला है, जिससे वहाँ पहुँचना थोड़ा असुविधाजनक हो जाता है।

आज हम 10 परिवारजन मंदिर दर्शन के लिए आए थे। दुर्भाग्य से, हमारे दो सदस्यों के ब्रांडेड जूते मंदिर परिसर से चोरी हो गए। यह अनुभव बहुत निराशाजनक था। आने वाले श्रद्धालुओं से निवेदन है कि अपने जूते-चप्पल की सुरक्षा पर विशेष ध्यान दें।

review_text.en:
The Swaminarayan Mandir in Pune is a true gem and a magnificent spiritual sanctuary for the city. It is an absolute blessing to have such a beautifully designed and serene place of worship so accessible. The temple's architecture is breathtaking, featuring intricate carvings and a majestic white structure that inspires awe and peace. Perched on a hill, it commands a huge area, offering a sense of grandeur and tranquility away from the city's hustle.

The entire complex is impeccably well-maintained and spotlessly clean, which greatly enhances the devotional experience. The large land allows for peaceful strolls and quiet reflection. The one significant challenge we faced was with parking. While there is a generous amount of space allocated for cars, the parking area is unpaved. During the rainy season, this turns extremely muddy and waterlogged, making it very difficult to navigate without getting dirty. It was a troublesome end to an otherwise perfect visit.

Despite the parking issue, the temple itself is a five-star experience. I highly recommend a visit for its spiritual ambiance, stunning design, and pristine upkeep. It is a must-see landmark in Pune.

review_text.en:
The BAPS Swaminarayan Mandir in Punawale–Narhe, Pune, is a serene and spiritually uplifting place that perfectly blends divine peace with architectural beauty. Managed by the Bochasanwasi Akshar Purushottam Swaminarayan Sanstha (BAPS), the temple stands as a symbol of devotion, discipline, and community service. As you enter the premises, you are welcomed by a calm and pure atmosphere, enhanced by beautifully carved idols and a well-maintained campus. The temple’s architecture, though simple compared to grand BAPS temples like those in Delhi or Ahmedabad, carries the same essence of purity and elegance, with intricate detailing that reflects traditional Indian craftsmanship.

Daily aarti, bhajans, and spiritual discourses are conducted with great devotion, offering visitors a moment of reflection and inner peace. The temple also promotes values such as harmony, respect, and selfless service through its youth and social activities. The premises are clean, organized, and family-friendly, making it an ideal place for both spiritual seekers and those looking for a peaceful retreat from city life.

During festivals like Diwali, Janmashtami, and Swaminarayan Jayanti, the temple comes alive with lights, devotion, and cultural programs that attract devotees from all over Pune. Overall, the BAPS Swaminarayan Mandir at Punawale–Narhe is not just a place of worship but a center of peace, positivity, and moral inspiration for all ages.

BAPS Shri Swaminarayan Mandir is a magnificent example of ancient Indian temple architecture, built entirely from hand-carved stones without the use of any steel or iron. The temple's walls and pillars feature intricate carvings of sculptures and artistry. It is constructed primarily using pink and red stone, which gives it a very grand appearance.
The area surrounding the temple creates a spiritual atmosphere that offers a great sense of peace and tranquility. The temple premises are exceptionally well-maintained and clean.
Facilities include free parking and access to drinking water, as well as rooms for resting.
This temple is a wonderful place to visit with family, particularly beautiful during the morning and evening hours. However, be very crowded on weekends.

review_text.en:
The BAPS Shri Swaminarayan Temple in Pune is truly a gem of serenity and spiritual beauty. Located in Ambegaon, just off the Mumbai–Bengaluru highway, it’s a peaceful escape from the city’s bustle. The temple’s architecture is breathtaking — intricately carved pink sandstone and marble work that reflect traditional Indian craftsmanship with modern precision.

As soon as you enter the temple premises, you feel a sense of calm and cleanliness that’s rare these days. The gardens and pathways are beautifully maintained, and volunteers are always courteous and helpful. The main sanctum, with the idols of Bhagwan Swaminarayan and other deities, radiates positive energy. The evening arti (prayer ceremony) is a must-attend — it’s deeply uplifting, with devotional chants and rhythmic prayers filling the air.

review_text.en: BAPS Swaminarayan Akshardham is a complex of Hindu mandirs and cultural centers built by the Bochasanwasi Akshar Purushottam Swaminarayan Sanstha (BAPS). Known for their elaborate architecture, these complexes are dedicated to Bhagwan Swaminarayan and serve as centers for worship, cultural preservation, and education. There are major locations in Gandhinagar (Gujarat, India), Delhi (India), and Robbinsville (New Jersey, USA).

BAPS Shri Swaminarayan Temple, also known as a BAPS Mandir, is a beautifully designed Hindu temple built by the Bochasanwasi Akshar Purushottam Swaminarayan Sanstha (BAPS). The temple is dedicated to Bhagwan Swaminarayan and reflects traditional Indian architecture with intricate carvings, peaceful surroundings, and a spiritually uplifting atmosphere.

These temples are known for:

Stunning stone and marble architecture

Intricate hand-carved designs

Calm, devotional environment

Cultural and spiritual activities like aarti, satsang, and festivals

Community service initiatives organized by BAPS

Visitors often come to experience the serene atmosphere, learn about Hindu culture, and admire the craftsmanship of the temple.

मंदिर वाकई बहुत भव्य है।
हम शाम के समय आए थे, और उस वक्त यहाँ फोटो लेना भी बहुत अच्छा लगता है।
बच्चों के खेलने के लिए भी यह जगह काफ़ी उपयुक्त है, और प्रवेश द्वार पर सार्वजनिक शौचालय भी है।

बस एक कमी यह है कि जूतों को रखने के लिए कोई तय जगह नहीं है — सब लोग जहाँ जगह मिलती है वहीं रख देते हैं।
स्टाफ का व्यवहार विनम्र था, लेकिन सीटी लगातार बजाने का मतलब समझ नहीं आया… क्या वे ट्रैफ़िक कंट्रोल कर रहे थे?
इसी वजह से एक स्टार कम।

review_text.en:
BAPS SHRI SWAMINARAYAN MANDIR (2025) All You Need to Know ...The BAPS Shri Swaminarayan Mandir in Pune is a traditional stone temple located in the Narhe suburb, known for its intricate architecture and cultural significance. It features handcrafted carvings, has a daily mid-day arti at 11:15 am, and offers vegetarian food and a gift shop. The temple is accessible to all, with amenities like a wheelchair-accessible entrance, restrooms, and parking.
Location and access
Address: Narhe suburb, Pune
Distance from Pune Junction: 14 kilometers
Transportation: Available via cab; volunteers guide visitors from the parking area
Parking: Free and paid options are available
Accessibility: Wheelchair accessible entrance, parking, and restrooms
Temple highlights
Architecture: Traditionally hand-carved stone temple built according to ancient Indian Shilpshastras
Key features:
23 Samrans, including seven large ones
140 carved pillars
109 Toran with 10,269 carved sculptures and deities
An Abhishek Mandapam for the ritual
Amenities:
Gift shop with books, pendrives, CDs/DVDs, and herbal products
Food shop serving all-vegetarian cuisine
Daily activities: Midday arti ceremony at 11:15 am
Visitor experience
Atmosphere: Peaceful, with an enriching cultural experience and a strong sense of reverence
Activities: Visitors can participate in darshan (seeing the deities) and other devotional activities
Staff: Volunteers and staff are described as warm and welcoming

review_text.en:
This mandir is known for its stunning, traditional architecture, intricately carved from pink sandstone and white marble without the use of steel.

Architectural Significance :

Design: Built in the Nagara-style (specifically Southern Shilpa Shaili) without the use of any steel.

Details: The temple is set upon 140 carved pillars and features 10,269 hand-carved sculptures that depict both Indian and Maharashtrian heritage.

Materials: Constructed using traditionally hand-carved pink sandstone and red stone from Rajasthan.

review_text.hi:
ण्यातील स्वामीनारायण मंदिर हे BAPS (Bochasanwasi Akshar Purushottam Swaminarayan Sanstha) द्वारे संचालित असून, ते नर्हे (Narhe) भागात आहे, जेथे सुंदर भारतीय स्थापत्यकलेचे दर्शन घडते; हे मंदिर भगवान स्वामीनारायणाला समर्पित असून, आध्यात्मिक शांती आणि सांस्कृतिक वारसा जपणारे एक प्रमुख ठिकाण आहे. हे मंदिर संगमरवरी आणि गुलाबी वाळूच्या दगडांनी बनवलेले असून, त्यात विविध देवतांच्या मूर्ती आणि कोरीवकाम आढळते

स्थान: नर्हे-आंबेगाव रोड, पुणे (NH 48 वर).
वैशिष्ट्ये: पारंपरिक नागर शैलीतील वास्तुकला, संगमरवरी आणि दगडी कोरीवकाम, शांत वातावरण, आणि विविध धार्मिक कार्यक्रम.
उद्देश: आध्यात्मिक मार्गदर्शन, सांस्कृतिक कार्यक्रम आणि सामाजिक सेवा प्रदान करणे.
वेळा: सकाळी आणि संध्याकाळी दर्शनासाठी उघडे असते (वेळापत्रक बदलू शकते, त्यामुळे प्रत्यक्ष भेट देण्यापूर्वी तपासणे उत्तम).

स्वामीनारायण मंदिर हे खरोखरच शांत आणि आध्यात्मिकदृष्ट्या उन्नत ठिकाण आहे.
मंदिराची वास्तुकला सुंदर, तपशीलवार आणि अतिशय चांगल्या प्रकारे राखलेली आहे. प्रवेश करताच तुम्हाला सर्वत्र शांतता आणि सकारात्मक ऊर्जा जाणवते. आजूबाजूचा परिसर स्वच्छ, सुव्यवस्थित आणि प्रसन्न आहे. दर्शनाचा अनुभव दिव्य आणि मन आणि आत्म्याला शांत करणारा आहे. मंदिराचा परिसर प्रशस्त आहे आणि ध्यान आणि शांत वेळेसाठी परिपूर्ण आहे. व्यवस्थापन आणि स्वयंसेवक सभ्य, शिस्तबद्ध आणि मदतगार आहेत. एकूण वातावरण भक्ती, शिस्त आणि शुद्धता प्रतिबिंबित करते.शहराच्या गोंगाटापासून दूर आंतरिक शांती मिळविण्यासाठी हे एक आदर्श ठिकाण आहे.

review_text.hi:
स्वामीनारायण मंदिर – शांति, भक्ति और दिव्यता का अद्भुत संगम
स्वामीनारायण मंदिर केवल एक धार्मिक स्थल नहीं, बल्कि मन, आत्मा और जीवन को सकारात्मक ऊर्जा से भर देने वाला पवित्र स्थान है। जैसे ही मंदिर परिसर में प्रवेश करते हैं, एक अलग ही शांति और सुकून का अनुभव होता है। मंदिर की वास्तुकला अत्यंत भव्य, सुंदर और आकर्षक है, जो भारतीय संस्कृति और आध्यात्मिक परंपरा को जीवंत रूप में प्रस्तुत करती है।
यहाँ का वातावरण बहुत ही स्वच्छ, अनुशासित और भक्तिमय है। सुबह और शाम की आरती के समय मंदिर में मौजूद ऊर्जा शब्दों में बयान नहीं की जा सकती। भजन, कीर्तन और मंत्रोच्चारण से मन स्वतः ही ध्यान और भक्ति में लीन हो जाता है। भगवान स्वामीनारायण की मूर्ति अत्यंत दिव्य और मन को आकर्षित करने वाली है।
मंदिर के सेवक और स्वयंसेवक बहुत ही विनम्र, सहयोगी और अनुशासित होते हैं, जो आने वाले हर श्रद्धालु का आदरपूर्वक मार्गदर्शन करते हैं। यहाँ बैठकर ध्यान करने, प्रार्थना करने और आत्मचिंतन के लिए शांत स्थान उपलब्ध है।
यह मंदिर बच्चों, युवाओं और बुजुर्गों—सभी के लिए आध्यात्मिक सीख और संस्कार देने का कार्य करता है। परिवार के साथ समय बिताने और मानसिक शांति प्राप्त करने के लिए यह स्थान अत्यंत उपयुक्त है।
जो भी व्यक्ति जीवन की भागदौड़ से दूर कुछ समय शांति और ईश्वर के सान्निध्य में बिताना चाहता है, उसे स्वामीनारायण मंदिर अवश्य आना चाहिए। यह स्थान मन को शुद्ध करता है और जीवन को नई दिशा देता है।
🙏 जय स्वामीनारायण 🙏

Swaminarayan Temple is a very huge and beautifully built temple, making it one of the most peaceful and spiritual places in Pune. We visited it on the first day of the New Year to begin the year with God’s blessings, and it truly felt like the perfect start.

The architecture is stunning, with intricate detailing and excellent craftsmanship. The temple is well maintained, and the volunteers also share short and informative details about the temple, which adds to the overall experience.

The surrounding garden area is also beautifully designed, but it needs better maintenance. Cleanliness in the outer areas should be improved. Parking space is quite large, but it needs proper construction and organization. Washrooms require better cleanliness and upgrades as well.

The canteen is available but definitely needs improvement in terms of quality and overall setup. It’s also important to note that maintaining cleanliness is not just the temple management’s responsibility—devotees should also ensure they keep the premises clean and respectful.

Important note: The temple remains closed for darshan during a specific time in the afternoon and reopens at 4 PM, so it’s advisable to check the timings before planning your visit.

Overall, Swaminarayan Temple is a wonderful place to seek blessings, experience beautiful architecture, and enjoy a calm spiritual atmosphere. It is surely one of the must-visit places in Pune. 🙏

review_text.en:
BAPS Shri Swaminarayan Mandir, Pune is one of the most peaceful and spiritually uplifting places in the city. The moment you enter the premises, you feel a deep sense of calm and positivity that instantly disconnects you from the chaos of daily life.
The temple architecture is absolutely stunning. Built with intricately carved pink sandstone, every pillar, dome, and sculpture reflects exceptional craftsmanship and devotion. The detailing is so fine that it feels like a living piece of art. The temple is very clean, well-organized, and beautifully maintained, which adds to the overall experience.
The atmosphere inside the mandir is serene and devotional. The darshan experience is well managed even during busy hours, and the volunteers are polite, disciplined, and helpful. The chanting and aarti create a deeply spiritual environment that brings peace to the mind and heart.
The surrounding gardens and open spaces are well landscaped, making it a great place to sit quietly and reflect. There is ample parking available, and facilities like clean washrooms and drinking water are well maintained. The location is slightly away from the city center, but the journey is completely worth it.
This temple is perfect for families, elders, and anyone seeking mental peace or spiritual growth. It is also a wonderful place for photography (outside areas) and learning about Indian culture and values.
Overall, BAPS Swaminarayan Mandir Pune is not just a religious place, but a spiritual retreat that leaves you feeling refreshed, positive, and grateful. Highly recommended for everyone visiting Pune.

We decided to visit this beautiful and large temple located in Narhe, Pune. We had been planning to come here for almost a year, and today we finally visited. The temple is very peaceful, well maintained, and truly impressive.

The Google Maps location is accurate, so reaching the temple is easy without any confusion.

Things to consider before visiting:

There is plenty of car and bike parking available. I even noticed tour and travel buses (Volvo) parked there, so parking is well managed.

Temple timings: 7:00 AM to 12:00 PM and 4:00 PM to 8:30 PM. Plan your visit accordingly.

If you visit during the scheduled time, you can also attend the aarti, which is a very calming experience.

The temple gets quite crowded on weekends, so expect some waiting time.

Overall, it is a must-visit place if you are looking for peace and spiritual calm in Pune.

review_text.en:
A Sanctuary of Stone and Silence: In-Depth Review of BAPS Shri Swaminarayan Mandir, Pune
Rating: ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ (4.8/5)
If you are looking for a place in Pune that offers a profound sense of peace while simultaneously leaving you awestruck with its architectural brilliance, the BAPS Shri Swaminarayan Mandir in Narhe-Ambegaon is the destination. It feels less like a typical temple visit and more like a spiritual retreat.
1. Architecture & Ambience
The moment you step off the bustling Mumbai-Bengaluru Highway and onto the temple grounds, the noise of the city fades away. The structure itself is a masterpiece of the ancient Shilpa Shastras. Built entirely from pink sandstone and red stone without a single piece of steel, the craftsmanship is incredible.
• The Carvings: Take a moment to look closely at the pillars and ceilings. The intricate carvings of peacocks, elephants, and deities are mesmerizing. It is visually similar to the grand Akshardham temples, albeit on a smaller scale.
• The Vibe: The atmosphere is incredibly sattvic (pure). Unlike many temples that can feel chaotic or rushed, this one maintains a strict discipline of silence, making it genuinely easy to meditate.
2. The Spiritual Experience
The main sanctum is spotlessly clean and air-conditioned/ventilated naturally by the stone design. The murtis (idols) of Bhagwan Swaminarayan and other deities are adorned with stunning fabrics and jewelry.
• Best Moment: Try to coincide your visit with the evening Aarti (usually around 7:00 PM). The rhythmic chanting and the sound of bells create an electric atmosphere that stays with you long after you leave.
3. The "Premvati" Food Court (A Must-Visit)
Do not leave without visiting the canteen (Premvati). It serves pure vegetarian, sattvic food (no onion, no garlic) that tastes homemade and is very hygienic.
• What to Eat: Their Khichdi-Kadhi combo is famous for a reason—it is comfort food at its best. The Vada Pav, Dhokla, and herbal tea are also excellent.
• Price: Very affordable. A family of four can eat well for a surprisingly low amount.
4. Practicalities & Facilities
• Parking: There is ample parking space for both cars and bikes, which is a huge relief in Pune. It is free and well-managed by volunteers.
• Cleanliness: The washrooms and shoe-keeping areas are better maintained than most malls. BAPS is known for its obsession with cleanliness, and it shows here.
• Accessibility: There are lifts and ramps available for the elderly or those who cannot climb stairs.
5. Important Constraints (Know Before You Go)
• No Photography Inside: This is the most important rule. You cannot take photos inside the main temple. Security will ask you to put your phone away. You can only take photos in the gardens and of the temple exterior.
• Dress Code: They are strict about modest clothing. Shoulders and knees must be covered. Avoid shorts, sleeveless tops, or short skirts, or you may be denied entry (or asked to wear a sarong provided by them).

review_text.en:
The Invisible Giant: A Masterclass in Spiritual Hide-and-Seek 🕵️‍♂️
Think you’ve missed it? Think again. This temple is a literal master of disguise, invisible from the road until you breach the gates and get hit by an immediate, high-definition "aura." It turns out this megalith is a sibling to the famous Akshardham, and the family resemblance is in the DNA—from the intricate door designs to a "subterranean sequel" floor beneath the main dome housing almost every deity imaginable. My initial skepticism was completely shattered by the sheer scale of the craftsmanship; it’s an architectural flex that makes your average building look like cardboard.
Beyond the stone, the premises are a bit of a pleasant fever dream, featuring a serene "embossed" garden, a modest outdoor gaming zone, and—miraculously—a massive parking lot that costs exactly zero rupees. There are even on-site restaurants for when the spiritual journey makes you hungry. My best advice for the uninitiated? Treat Google Maps like a sacred text and follow it blindly. Your eyes will lie to you until you’re at the gate, but once you’re in, the effort is worth every penny!!!

review_text.en:
Bochasanwasi Akshar Purushottam Swaminarayan Sanstha (BAPS) is a Hindu denomination within the Swaminarayan Sampradaya. It was formed in 1905 by Shastri Yagnapurushdas (Shastriji Maharaj) following his conviction that Swaminarayan remained present on earth through a lineage of gurus starting with Gunatitanand Swami. As of August 2016, Mahant Swami Maharaj is the 6th guru and president of BAPS.
The basis for the formation of BAPS was Shastriji Maharaj's conviction that Swaminarayan remained present on earth through a lineage of Gunatit Gurus (perfect devotee), starting with Gunatitanand Swami, one of Swaminarayan's most prominent disciples and that Swaminarayan and his choicest devotee, Gunatitanand Swami, were ontologically, Purushottam and Akshar, respectively. According to the BAPS-tradition, Shastriji Maharaj had understood this from his guru, Bhagatji Maharaj, who had Gunatitanand Swami as his guru.
Followers of BAPS believe that the Ekantik dharma that Swaminarayan desired to establish is embodied and propagated by the Ekantik Satpurush (a most sublime saint), the Gunatit Guru. According to Shastriji Maharaj, Swaminarayan had "expressly designated" the Gunatit Guru to spiritually guide the satsang (spiritual fellowship) while instructing his nephews to help manage the administration of the fellowship within their respective dioceses: 610  As Kim notes, "For BAPS devotees, the dual murtis in the original Swaminarayan temples imply that Swaminarayan did install a murti of himself alongside the murti of his ideal bhakta or Guru".
Shastriji Maharaj sought to publicly reveal his ideas, and to worship Gunatitanand as the abode of Purushottam, c.q. Swaminarayan.However, his views were rejected by the sadhus of the Vadtal and Ahmedabad dioceses. For the sadhus of the Vadtal diocese, the idea that Swaminarayan had appointed Gunatitanand as his spiritual successor, instead of the two acharyas, was a heretical teaching, and they "refused to worship what they considered to be a human being." Shastriji Maharaj left Vadtal with five swamis and the support of about 150 devotees.

review_text.en:
I recently visited BAPS Shri Swaminarayan Mandir in Pune around 10 in the morning, and it was an absolutely beautiful experience. Parking was conveniently available, which made the visit easy and stress-free. The mandir itself is stunning, with incredible architecture and a very peaceful atmosphere.

The place is extremely clean, quiet, and well-maintained, which adds to the overall spiritual and calming experience. Photography and phone usage are restricted inside the main mandir area, which helps maintain the sanctity and peaceful environment. The entire complex is very photogenic from the outside, and every corner looks thoughtfully designed and visually pleasing.

I truly loved visiting this mandir. It’s a must-visit place if you are in Pune, whether for spiritual reasons or simply to admire the beauty and peaceful surroundings.

The temple is known for its stunning architecture, with detailed carvings on pillars and walls that truly reflect Indian craftsmanship. It is built using stone without steel and has thousands of intricate sculptures, making it visually breathtaking.
BAPS Swaminarayan Sanstha
The entire परिसर is very clean and well-maintained, and the atmosphere is शांत and spiritual. It’s a perfect place for meditation, prayer, and spending peaceful time away from city noise.
There is ample space for parking, a garden area, and even small facilities like a canteen and seating areas, making it ideal for families.

review_text.en: The BAPS Swaminarayan Temple near Jambhulwadi (Narhe) is an absolute architectural marvel. The pink sandstone carvings are incredibly detailed, and the entire space feels vast, pleasant, and soul-soothing. One of the best parts is that there are no entry tickets—it is completely free for everyone. While it’s very close to the highway and easily accessible, public bus connectivity right to the gate is limited, so it’s best to travel by auto or private vehicle. There is ample parking available. A must-visit spot in Pune for anyone who appreciates grand architecture and a peaceful environment!

The pink sandstone carvings are incredibly detailed, and the entire space feels vast, pleasant, and soul-soothing. One of the best parts is that there are no entry tickets—it is completely free for everyone. While it’s very close to the highway and easily accessible, public bus connectivity right to the gate is limited, so it’s best to travel by auto or private vehicle. There is ample parking available. A must-visit spot in Pune for anyone who appreciates grand architecture and a peaceful environment!

Serious security risk—avoid carrying valuables!

Do not trust the "security" here.

review_text.hi:
Visited BAPS Swaminarayan Mandir Narhe recently and it stands out as one of the most serene places in the Ambegaon–Narhe area. Despite being close to the busy Mumbai–Bangalore Highway, the moment you enter the mandir परिसर, there is a noticeable calmness and positive energy.

The temple is not overly crowded like some central Pune temples, which actually makes it ideal for peaceful darshan and meditation. The marble idols and the main sanctum are very well maintained, and the cleanliness across the area is impressive. The volunteers (sevaks) are polite and guide visitors properly, especially during aarti time.

Evenings here feel especially soothing with the lighting and calm environment. There is decent parking space available nearby, which is a big advantage in this area. The surrounding locality is still developing, so the mandir feels slightly away from chaos - a good thing if you’re looking for quiet spiritual time.

Overall, a very well-managed and peaceful place for devotees and anyone looking to spend some peaceful moments in Pune.

review_text.en:
We recently visited the BAPS Shri Swaminarayan Mandir in Katraj with the family, and it was a wonderful experience overall. The premises are sprawling and exceptionally clean, which is always a relief when you are out with kids. My daughter had plenty of open space to run around and play, making it a very family-friendly spot.
The highlight was definitely the darshan of Shri Radha Krishna. The idols and the altar are stunningly beautiful, and the entire atmosphere feels peaceful. Visiting in the evening is a treat because the lighting and the cool breeze across the huge grounds give a real sense of pleasure and calm.
If there is one area for improvement, it is the shoe management. There weren’t great arrangements for footwear, so most shoes were just lying around in a bit of a mess. Also, keep in mind that the temple is a bit of a distance from the Katraj bus stand. We didn't have much trouble getting there though, as sharing auto-rickshaws are easily available. It is a must-visit if you are looking for a spiritual yet relaxing evening in Pune.

If you’re looking for a quiet escape to find inner peace, this is definitely the place to visit. The architecture, cleanliness, and overall atmosphere make it a must-visit spot in Pune.
Also, try to attend the Sandhya Aarti around 7 PM—it’s a truly divine and soothing experience that adds even more to the peaceful vibe of the temple.

Enough parking for 4W and 2W.

The temple is very clean and well maintained, and the discipline and management inside are impressive. Spending time here gave a feeling of peace and positivity.

It is a wonderful place to visit with family and a perfect destination for spiritual relaxation and devotion.

Visited BAPS Shri Swaminarayan Temple and it was truly a peaceful and spiritual experience. The temple is extremely well-maintained and hygienic, even though it gets quite crowded in the evenings.

The architecture and intricate detailing of the temple are absolutely beautiful. The murtis (idols) are stunning, and I really appreciated how each one had its own description/story mentioned, which made the visit even more meaningful and immersive.

There’s a very calm and peaceful energy here — it’s the kind of place where you can simply sit alone for hours and feel at peace. Definitely worth visiting for both the spiritual atmosphere and the beautiful craftsmanship.

Overall, Swaminarayan Temple is a must visit place for anyone seeking devotion, peace of mind, and a positive spiritual experience.

review_text.en:
We recently visited the BAPS Shri Swaminarayan Temple, and it turned out to be a truly refreshing and peaceful experience.

The temple itself is absolutely beautiful, with intricate carvings and stunning architecture that reflect fine craftsmanship and devotion. Every corner feels thoughtfully designed, making it a visually and spiritually uplifting place to visit.

Parking facilities are well-managed, with ample space available for visitors, which makes the visit hassle free. Around the temple area , there are a few food stalls offering snacks and refreshments, adding to the overall convenience.

Photography inside the temple is restricted to maintain its sanctity and peaceful atmosphere, which is understandable. However, there are plenty of photogenic spots outside where you can capture the beauty of the temple and its surroundings.

Overall, it’s a serene, well-maintained place that offers both spiritual calm and aesthetic beauty , definitely worth a visit.

review_text.en:
BAPS Shri Swaminarayan Temple – Review

BAPS Shri Swaminarayan Temple is a beautiful an;d peaceful spiritual destination that attracts visitors of all ages. The temple's magnificent architecture, serene atmosphere, and well-maintained surroundings make it an ideal place for prayer, relaxation, and family outings.

Highlights:

No entry fee is charged, making it accessible to everyone.
Lift and wheelchair facilities are available, ensuring convenient access for senior citizens, differently-abled visitors, and families with young children. The temple is surrounded by lush greenery, creating a calm and refreshing environment.
Visitors can enjoy stunning sunrise and sunset views, which add to the beauty and tranquility
Light show is organized in the evening, offering an engaging and visually appealing experience.
A restaurant within the premises provides a convenient option for refreshments and meals.
There is a dedicated play area for children, featuring inflatable slides and other attractions that make the visit enjoyable for families.
The temple campus is spacious and well-maintained, though some walking is required to explore the entire complex comfortably.

Overall, BAPS Shri Swaminarayan Temple offers a wonderful blend of spirituality, architecture, natural beauty, and family-friendly facilities. Whether you are visiting for worship, sightseeing, or spending quality time with family, it provides a memorable and peaceful experience.

No photography is permitted inside the temple.

review_text.en:
My Skechers worth ₹10k are gone – Beware of Organized Theft

I am deeply disappointed to report the theft of my Skechers shoes (worth ₹10,000) from the temple premises. Upon inquiring, I discovered that this isn't an isolated incident—there is an active gang of kids targeting pilgrims' valuable and branded footwear. Shockingly, these incidents have been reported multiple times in the past, yet absolutely no action has been taken.
While the temple has a "Shoe Rack" signboard, the actual facility is completely inadequate. There was no space, forcing me—like other devotees—to leave my shoes outside the entrance.

I entirely blame the temple management for this loss. Being fully aware of recurring thefts and doing nothing to increase security, install functional CCTV is pure negligence. If you are visiting, do not wear expensive footwear, as the management takes zero responsibility for the safety of your belongings.

review_text.en:
Swaminarayan Temple at Narhe is a divine and peaceful place that offers a refreshing escape from the busy pace of city life. Built in a spacious open area the temple complex is beautifully maintained and provides a serene environment for prayer meditation and relaxation. The moment you enter the premises you can feel a sense of calm that makes it an ideal place to spend a few peaceful hours with family.

The architecture of the temple is impressive and thoughtfully designed with intricate detailing and a grand appearance that reflects the rich traditions of the Swaminarayan faith. The temple premises are clean well organized and managed efficiently which enhances the overall visitor experience.

One of the biggest advantages of this temple is the vast open space surrounding it. There is ample room to walk around sit quietly and enjoy the peaceful atmosphere. Families with children will especially appreciate the dedicated play areas where kids can have fun while adults spend time exploring the temple or simply relaxing.

Parking is never a concern as the temple has ample space to accommodate a large number of vehicles even during weekends and festivals. The open surroundings fresh air and well maintained gardens make it a wonderful place to refresh your mind and rejuvenate yourself.

Whether you are visiting for spiritual reasons architectural appreciation or simply to spend some quality time in a peaceful environment this temple offers a fulfilling experience. It is one of the best places in the Narhe area for anyone looking to combine devotion relaxation and family time in a single visit.

💡 ट्रैवल टिप (Travel Tip): शनिवार, रविवार या छुट्टी के दिन यहाँ बहुत भीड़ होती है, जिसकी वजह से कात्रज-आंबेगाव हाईवे पर ट्रैफिक जाम हो सकता है। दर्शन करने के बाद भारी ट्रैफिक में खुद गाड़ी चलाना और पार्किंग ढूंढना काफी थका देने वाला होता है। साथ ही अगर आप यहाँ से आगे महाबलेश्वर, लोनावला या कोंकण की ट्रिप प्लान कर रहे हैं, तो खुद ड्राइव करने का टेंशन छोड़ दें। आरामदायक सफर के लिए आप MyCabLo Car Rental Services से प्रोफेशनल ड्राइवर के साथ Premium Force Urbania, Innova Crysta या Ertiga बुक कर सकते हैं। एसी गाड़ी में आराम से सफर का आनंद लें!

review_text.en:
The BAPS Swaminarayan Temple is a magnificent place that combines spirituality, culture, and stunning architecture. The intricate carvings, beautifully maintained surroundings, and peaceful atmosphere create a truly divine experience. The temple premises are clean, organized, and welcoming to visitors of all backgrounds. The serene environment makes it an ideal place for prayer, meditation, and spending quality time with family. The volunteers and staff are polite and helpful, ensuring a pleasant visit. The temple also offers insights into Indian culture, values, and traditions through its exhibitions and activities. Overall, it is a must-visit destination for anyone seeking peace, devotion, and architectural beauty.
Highly recommended for spiritual seekers, families, and architecture enthusiasts.

review_text.en:
I recently visited the BAPS Shri Swaminarayan Mandir in Pune, and it is truly an experience that stays with you. Located conveniently off the Mumbai-Bengaluru Highway, it serves as a perfect spiritual oasis away from the city's hustle and bustle.

The architecture is absolutely breathtaking. Knowing that this massive structure was built entirely of hand-carved pink and red sandstone without the use of any steel is mind-blowing. The intricate carvings on every pillar and wall showcase incredible craftsmanship that feels more like a divine museum than just a place of worship.

The entire premises are spotlessly clean and exceptionally well-managed. The gardens are lush, green, and very well-maintained, providing a perfect space to sit and reflect. If you get a chance, visit in the evening; the way the temple is illuminated is nothing short of magical.

The facilities are very convenient for visitors. They have clean washrooms and a canteen that serves delicious, affordable vegetarian food. The parking is free and spacious, though it can get busy on weekends, so try to arrive early. Just a friendly reminder to dress modestly to respect the sanctity of the place and keep an eye on your personal belongings, especially your footwear at the designated stands.

The volunteers here are very disciplined, humble, and helpful, which adds to the overall positive and peaceful energy of the temple. Whether you are looking for a place for quiet meditation or just want to admire some world-class Indian architecture, this place is a must-visit. I am already looking forward to my next visit!
